must_have_breakdown.skill: System Requirements writing and System Architecture Design
RADAR System (End User function) and Product knowledge
Experience on system service function (Diagnostics, Flashability, FOTA, etc) development
Basic Functional Safety and Cyber Security knowledge
Experience in RADAR function validation (HIL and vehicle environment)
Requirement management tools DOORs, Code Beamer and configuration management tools such as CoreALM

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce Copado DevOps/Release Engineer with zero overlap with the automotive radar systems engineering role. Every single must-have — RADAR system knowledge, ASPICE, ISO26262, Embedded Systems, CAN/LIN/Ethernet/LVDS, Functional Safety, DOORS/CodeBeamer, Catia Magic — is completely absent from the resume. The candidate's entire career is Salesforce CRM platform deployments and CI/CD pipelines in telecom and enterprise software. There is no crossover to automotive systems engineering, ADAS, or embedded development. Do not shortlist — this is a domain mismatch, not a seniority mismatch.
strengths: COPADO DEVOPS DEPTH: 4+ years of hands-on Copado CI/CD pipeline ownership across SFP and Metadata formats with a measurable 30% reduction in deployment time — strong signal within Salesforce DevOps domain.

RELEASE GOVERNANCE: Led enterprise-level Salesforce release management at Atos Syntel for telecom clients with 24/7 production support, risk assessments, and hotfix coordination — demonstrates senior-level operational maturity.

CERTIFICATIONS BREADTH: Holds four Copado certifications (Fundamentals I and II in both Metadata and Source Format) plus Salesforce Admin and Platform Developer I — well-credentialed within the Salesforce ecosystem.
gaps: ZERO RADAR/AUTOMOTIVE DOMAIN EXPERIENCE: The role requires deep RADAR system product knowledge, HIL and vehicle validation experience, and automotive-specific protocols (CAN/LIN/Ethernet/LVDS). The candidate has no exposure to any of these — this is a fundamental domain disqualification.

NO EMBEDDED OR SYSTEMS ENGINEERING BACKGROUND: The JD demands ASPICE, ISO26262, Functional Safety, Cyber Security, DOORS, CodeBeamer, and Catia Magic. The candidate's entire skillset is cloud-based Salesforce CRM. There is no pathway to bridge this gap without a complete career change.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in the resume; no JD maximum specified, so no penalty applied.
Candidate's most recent role is listed as Chennai, which matches the JD location requirement.
Candidate has 6+ years of IT experience, exceeding the minimum threshold of half the 4-year relevant experience requirement.
JD specifies 'Any' gender preference, so no penalty is applied.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD, so no adjustment is applied.

availability_reason: Current role at Trimble Inc. is listed as March 2025 to Present with no end date or notice period stated.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce Release Engineer / Salesforce DevOps Lead
rare_assets: Dual-format Copado certification coverage (both SFP and Metadata Format at Fundamentals I and II levels) — uncommon combination in the Salesforce DevOps market.
self_reported_unverified: 30% reduction in deployment time — no baseline metrics, team size, or audit trail provided to substantiate this claim.
Certificate of Appreciation for Trimble Console Platform — award described without scope, delivery criteria, or independent validation.
Bronze Accolade Award at Eviden/Atos Group — described as 'exceptional contribution to organisational activities' with no specific technical outcome linked.
interview_flags: Probe whether the candidate has any awareness of automotive or embedded systems — even at a hobbyist or academic level — to rule out any hidden domain crossover.
Clarify the actual scale of deployments managed: number of orgs, user counts, and release frequency to validate the seniority claim.
Ask how the 30% deployment time reduction was measured — what was the baseline, what changed, and who validated the outcome.
Explore the MBA in HR Management (December 2025) — is the candidate pivoting away from engineering, and does this affect long-term retention in a technical role?
Verify gap period: internship ended August 2020, next role started March 2021 — clarify what the candidate did during this 7-month gap.
